source.title: Requirement for hippocampal CA3 NMDA receptors in associative memory recall

confidence_rationale: High-quality, mechanistically decisive mouse genetics in Science: a region-specific CA3 NMDA-receptor knockout selectively impairs recall from an INCOMPLETE cue set while sparing full-cue recall -- the canonical causal demonstration of hippocampal pattern completion. This is the biological substrate MECH-338 names for cue-driven, context-tagged retrieval. Held at 0.78: rodent model, and 'pattern completion of a stored representation' -> 'cue-addressed CandidateRule retrieval' is a substrate-plausibility mapping, not a behavioural spec.
failure_signatures: CA3-NR1 knockout mice show normal spatial memory with a full cue set but a selective deficit when retrieval is induced by an INCOMPLETE/partial cue set; CA1 place cells stay normal in full-cue environments but degrade under partial-cue removal -- direct causal evidence that a dedicated recurrent substrate completes a stored pattern from a partial cue. A retrieval architecture with no partial-cue completion stage would not reproduce the dissociation.

The deficit is specifically in recall from degraded input, not in encoding or in full-cue performance -- a model that conflated 'cue match' with simple feature lookup (no attractor/completion dynamics) mis-specifies the substrate MECH-338 depends on.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Genetic ablation of NMDA receptors restricted to CA3 pyramidal cells in adult mice leaves full-cue spatial memory and CA1 place fields intact but selectively impairs (i) recall when only an incomplete set of spatial cues is available and (ii) CA1 place-field stability under partial-cue conditions. CA3 recurrent collateral plasticity is therefore causally required for pattern completion -- retrieval of a complete stored representation from a partial input.
mapping.ree_translation: Provides the biological substrate MECH-338 posits for the 'select' face: cue-driven retrieval of a context-tagged CandidateRule is, mechanistically, pattern completion -- a partial cue (a reinstated context feature, a caregiver signal) reactivates the complete stored candidate via attractor-like recurrent dynamics. It anchors MECH-338's dependence on MECH-269 (a context must be discriminably represented before a partial cue can complete to it) and explains how a cue that is NOT the rule's content can still address the rule.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Mouse hippocampal spatial memory, not rule retrieval; 'completing a stored memory pattern from a partial cue' maps onto 'a cue addresses a context-tagged CandidateRule' by structural analogy at the substrate level, not as a behavioural or representational identity. REE does not implement a CA3 module; this grounds the PLAUSIBILITY and shape (attractor completion, not lookup) of the MECH-338 mechanism, not an implementation.
mapping.source_context: Region-specific conditional-knockout mice (CA3-restricted NR1 deletion); Morris water maze with full vs. partial cue sets plus in vivo CA1 place-cell recording.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality very high: decisive, well-replicated causal genetics in a top venue. Mapping fidelity good: the partial-cue completion dissociation is structurally the MECH-338 mechanism. Transfer risk moderate: rodent spatial memory -> abstract candidate-rule retrieval is a substrate-plausibility analogy, and REE implements no CA3 module.
